name: KrillApp.Trigger.IncomingWebHook
title: Incoming WebHook
description: Registers an HTTP endpoint at /incoming/{path} on the Krill server. When an HTTP request matching the configured path and method is received, the webhook node completes and every node observing it as a source is invoked. This enables external systems to trigger Krill automations via HTTP calls.
shortDescription: Fires when an HTTP request hits its configured endpoint; observing nodes are invoked.
llmPurpose: Fires when an HTTP request is received at the configured /incoming/{path} endpoint, invoking every node that observes it as a source.
llmBehavior: completes on match; observers are invoked
exposes an API endpoint on the server
llmInputs: HTTP request to /incoming/{path} with configured method
llmOutputs: fires on matching request; all observing nodes are invoked
llmPromptHints: trigger this from an external API
run this when I send an HTTP request
create an endpoint for this automation
llmCreationHints: Create an IncomingWebHook when the user wants to trigger automation from an external HTTP call. Requires a path string and HTTP method. Wire actions to observe the webhook: list it in their sources with SOURCE_INVOKED.

llmConnectionHints.role: entry-point trigger whose observers are invoked on incoming HTTP request
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observed-side: actions list this webhook in their sources with SOURCE_INVOKED (parent-default wiring covers nodes dropped under it).

llmExamples: Create a webhook endpoint that triggers a data collection.
Set up an API endpoint so I can trigger this from my CI pipeline.
Listen for incoming POST requests to run this automation.
